question.text: An exporter who will receive EUR and wants to convert to INR should hedge by:

answer.text: Selling EURINR futures or buying EURINR put options
Buying EURINR futures or buying EURINR call options
Selling USDINR futures or buying USDINR put options
Buying EURUSD futures or buying EURUSD call options

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An exporter receiving EUR faces the risk of EUR depreciation against INR. To hedge: sell EURINR futures (locks in the selling rate) or buy EURINR puts (insurance against EUR fall while keeping upside if EUR rises). **The Trap:** An importer PAYING in EUR would buy futures/calls. The exporter needs to SELL the foreign currency. **Cross-Exam Context:** Hedging direction is fundamental to Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember: Receive foreign currency = Sell futures/Buy puts. Pay foreign currency = Buy futures/Buy calls.
